task: wire the SemanticBus into the live server
input: 10 ADR-115 semantic primitives were implemented + unit-tested but emitted nothing at runtime, despite --semantic defaulting on.
output: semantic_tick_task drives the bus from the same broadcast tap the MQTT bridge uses; events surface on the WS stream.
critique: Classic dead-wiring: SemanticBus::new/bus.tick() were called ONLY in #[cfg(test)]; main.rs never instantiated it. Same failure class as #872 (MQTT flags on a separate Args) and #803 (count discarded). Lesson: a passing test suite does not prove the feature is reachable from main.

task: publish semantic primitives as Home-Assistant MQTT entities
input: The full discovery/state/privacy/Matter scaffolding for all 10 primitives existed and was tested, but had no live routing from the bus to the publisher.
output: Typed broadcast::Sender<SemanticEvent>; a second select! arm maps SemanticKind->EntityKind and publishes via the existing StateEncoder.
critique: Scaffolding-without-routing is dead-wiring's cousin: the pieces exist but nothing connects them. The module's own docs even described the intended channel. Reuse the existing tap instead of inventing a new path.

task: fix WS-subscriber gate dropping MQTT semantic events
input: semantic_tick_task skipped the whole loop when no WebSocket client was connected, so an HA-only pilot (MQTT, no browser) got zero events.
output: Guard only the WS-JSON send on receiver_count; the MQTT path always runs.
critique: A reviewer (CodeRabbit) caught this. The bug was exactly the target deployment. Lesson: don't gate side-channel B on the liveness of side-channel A.

task: persist learned semantic baselines across restarts
input: ElderlyInactivityAnomaly's longest_idle baseline was uptime-only; every reboot reset it to the 30-min floor and spammed false inactivity anomalies for hours.
output: semantic::persistence stores only the learned scalars (not session timers) to data/semantic_baselines.json; restore floors the value at the 30-min safety minimum.
critique: Persist only long-lived LEARNED values, never session-relative FSM timers (which correctly reset). Floor restored safety values so a stale/corrupt file can't make a gate fire too eagerly.

task: drop a flawed adaptive presence threshold under review
input: My adaptive presence threshold had two real defects a reviewer surfaced: loaded models bypassed it, and it couldn't bootstrap in a noisy empty room (the floor only learned while absent).
output: Removed the adaptive threshold entirely; kept only the solid persistence half; restored presence = sm > 0.03.
critique: When review surfaces a genuine DESIGN defect (not a nit), cut the feature from the PR and ship the solid half — don't patch a broken mechanism under pressure. The honest move strengthened the PR.

task: node->zone model to unlock bed_exit / bathroom / multi_room
input: 3 zone-gated primitives stayed dormant because nothing populated RawSnapshot.active_zones; --semantic-zones-file existed only on the unused lib cli::Args.
output: semantic::zones::ZoneMap loads a zone-centric JSON; build_semantic_snapshot resolves active_zones from per-node presence, excluding stale nodes.
critique: Same dead-wiring flag (a flag with no loader). Resolve zones from the per-node breakdown and exclude stale nodes so a frozen offline node can't keep a zone occupied.

task: make edge-vitals (mmWave) nodes drive zone presence
input: active_zones trusted node classification.presence, but edge-vitals-only nodes (C6+mmWave, the best bathroom sensor) never ran the CSI debounce, so they stayed absent and their zone never activated.
output: Set current_motion_level straight from the firmware-debounced vitals.presence/motion in the edge-vitals path.
critique: A reviewer caught a real product gap: the BEST presence sensor for the bathroom was silently excluded from zones. Validate that every data SOURCE feeds the field a feature depends on.

task: resolve a cross-PR conflict on a shared hotspot function
input: PR #5 (persistence) merged into main and modified semantic_tick_task; the still-open PR #3 (zones) also modified it -> merge conflict.
output: Rebased #3 onto the updated main; resolved the one conflict (keep both mod declarations), verified both features coexist, force-pushed.
critique: When two PRs touch the same hotspot function, rebase the second onto main after the first merges; git auto-merges non-overlapping edits and the conflict is usually trivial. Test both features coexist before force-pushing.

task: audit real maturity before claiming a capability works
input: Asked whether RuView is valuable / pilot-ready. Marketing said 'vital signs from a $9 ESP32'.
output: Dispatched audits: presence is production-grade; WiFi heart-rate is scaffold (not even linked into the server); credible vitals depend on the $15 mmWave radar whose firmware does the work.
critique: Audit by tracing the data path + grepping todo!/unimplemented!/cfg(test) BEFORE believing a capability. The valuable IP was in a $15 sensor, not the $9 narrative.

input: User asked if turbovec would potentiate RuView.
output: Concluded: real tech, but wrong scale for the edge product (re-ID needs ~hundreds of 128-dim vectors, not millions); the bottleneck is CSI DSP, not vector search. Defer to a fleet/scale phase.
critique: Evaluate a new dependency against the ACTUAL bottleneck and scale. A 16x memory win at 10M vectors is irrelevant when the edge has a few hundred. Right tool, wrong scale = premature.

task: position RuView for the Brazilian market
input: Where is the realizable value in BR?
output: Non-clinical elderly-care/ILPI over Home Assistant; gates are ANATEL homologation, LGPD (edge/no-cloud is an advantage), and avoiding ANVISA by NOT claiming a medical device.
critique: For a privacy/health-adjacent edge product in BR: lead with the non-clinical 'ambient assistive' frame to dodge ANVISA; the no-camera/on-device story is an LGPD asset; ANATEL is the hardware gate.
